name: flow-attention-refresh
description: 注意力刷新协议。在关键时刻强制读取核心文档，防止目标遗忘。被 flow-dev, flow-ralph 等命令引用。
capabilities: Defines 4 attention refresh protocols triggered at key workflow moments
Forces reading of BRAINSTORM.md at flow entry to align with original intent
Ensures TASKS.md is read before each task to clarify DoD and requirements
Integrates with flow-dev and flow-ralph for autonomous iteration loops
author: Dimon94
securitySummary: This is a pure documentation skill with zero attack surface. All 35 static findings are false positives: backticks flagged as shell execution are markdown code delimiters; cryptographic algorithm flags are misidentified Chinese characters and text; high entropy flag is normal documentation text. The skill defines when to read local project files (BRAINSTORM.md, TASKS.md, ERROR_LOG.md) and contains no executable code, network calls, command execution, or file writing.
valueStatement: When working on long tasks with many tool calls, AI assistants often forget the original goal as it gets pushed out of the context window. This skill provides a protocol that forces reading of core documents at critical moments to keep the project goal front and center.
limitations: This is a protocol definition, not an executable tool - it must be referenced by other skills
Only defines when to read files, does not execute any actions itself
Requires other flow-* commands to trigger the refresh protocols

useCases.title: Maintain focus on feature goals
useCases.description: When building complex features across many iterations, stay aligned with the original feature goal and acceptance criteria.

useCases.title: Prevent context drift
useCases.description: Teams using Claude Code or similar AI assistants can prevent the AI from drifting away from the original requirements.

useCases.title: Ensure test-driven development
useCases.description: QA teams can enforce that each task starts by re-reading the test definition before implementation.

promptTemplates.title: Flow Entry Refresh
promptTemplates.prompt: Before we begin, read BRAINSTORM.md to confirm our success criteria, then read TASKS.md to understand the current phase objectives. What are we trying to achieve?

promptTemplates.title: Task Start Refresh
promptTemplates.prompt: Read TASKS.md for the current task T### paragraph. What are the DoD requirements? What files do we need to touch?

promptTemplates.title: Ralph Iteration Refresh
promptTemplates.scenario: Starting a new iteration in the Ralph loop
promptTemplates.prompt: Read TASKS.md to find the first incomplete task. Read ERROR_LOG.md to see any recent errors. What should we do next and what should we avoid?

promptTemplates.title: Error Recovery Refresh
promptTemplates.scenario: Recovering from a test or build failure
promptTemplates.prompt: Record the error to ERROR_LOG.md, then read it along with the current TASKS.md task definition. What is the root cause and what is our fix strategy?

bestPractices: Run Protocol 1 (flow entry) at the start of every development session to realign with project goals
Before implementing any task, always execute Protocol 2 to read the exact DoD requirements
Use Protocol 3 in Ralph iterations to learn from previous errors and avoid repeating mistakes
antiPatterns: Skipping document reads because you remember the goal - context drift is real
Starting tasks without reading the DoD - leads to rework when requirements are misunderstood
Ignoring ERROR_LOG.md during recovery - causes repeated mistakes and wasted time

faq.question: Which AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: This skill is designed for Claude Code CLI and any Claude Code-compatible environment. The protocol can be applied to Claude and Codex with minor adjustments.

faq.question: How many files can this skill read?
faq.answer: The skill reads local project documentation files. There is no practical limit - it can read any number of files as needed by the protocol.

faq.question: Does this skill integrate with other flow-* commands?
faq.answer: Yes. It integrates with flow-dev, flow-ralph, and all flow-* commands. The protocols are referenced in their respective command definitions.

faq.question: Is user data sent to external servers?
faq.answer: No. This skill only reads local project documentation files. No data leaves the local development environment.

faq.question: What if ERROR_LOG.md does not exist?
faq.answer: The protocols handle missing files gracefully. For Protocol 3, simply skip reading ERROR_LOG.md if it does not exist.

faq.question: How is this different from simple reminder prompts?
faq.answer: This is a formal protocol with defined trigger points and actions. It prevents the I remember illusion that leads to context drift in long-running AI-assisted sessions.
